showFullCode.title: Show Complete File
showFullCode.description: Show complete file for code, with bootstrap and error handling included.

bitly_url.description: The unique bit.ly URL for this GIF

create_datetime.description: The date this GIF was added to the GIPHY database.

embded_url.description: A URL used for embedding this GIF

featured_tags.description: An array of featured tags for this GIF (Note: Not available when using the Public Beta Key)

import_datetime.description: The creation or upload date from this GIF's source.

rating.description: The MPAA-style rating for this content. Examples include Y, G, PG, PG-13 and R

slug.description: The unique slug used in this GIF's URL

source.description: The page on which this GIF was found

source_post_url.description: The URL of the webpage on which this GIF was found.

source_tld.description: The top level domain of the source URL.

tags.description: An array of tags for this GIF (Note: Not available when using the Public Beta Key)

trending_datetime.description: The date on which this gif was marked trending, if applicable.

update_datetime.description: The date on which this GIF was last updated.

url.description: The unique URL for this GIF

username.description: The username this GIF is attached to, if applicable

frames.description: The number of frames in this GIF.

height.description: The height of this GIF in pixels.

mp4.description: The URL for this GIF in .MP4 format.

mp4_size.description: The size in bytes of the .MP4 file corresponding to this GIF.

size.description: The size of this GIF in bytes.

url.description: The publicly-accessible direct URL for this GIF.

webp.description: The URL for this GIF in .webp format.

webp_size.description: The size in bytes of the .webp file corresponding to this GIF.

width.description: The width of this GIF in pixels.

Meta.title: Meta
Meta.description: The Meta Object contains basic information regarding the request, whether it was successful, and the response given by the API.  Check `responses` to see a description of types of response codes the API might give you under different cirumstances.

response_id.description: A unique ID paired with this response from the API.

Pagination.title: Pagination
Pagination.description: The Pagination Object contains information relating to the number of total results available as well as the number of results fetched and their relative positions.

User.title: User
User.description: The User Object contains information about the user associated with a GIF and URLs to assets such as that user's avatar image, profile, and more.

avatar_url.description: The URL for this user's avatar image.

banner_url.description: The URL for the banner image that appears atop this user's profile page.

display_name.description: The display name associated with this user (contains formatting the base username might not).

profile_url.description: The URL for this user's profile.

twitter.description: The Twitter username associated with this user, if applicable.

username.description: The username associated with this user.

Images.title: Images
Images.description: An object containing data for various available formats and sizes of this GIF.

Type.title: Type
Type.description: Type of the gif. By default, this is almost always gif

Nodes.Text: Giphy API
Official Giphy Documentation: [https://developers.giphy.com/docs/](https://developers.giphy.com/docs/)

You must have Python ```3.7+``` installed on your system to install and run this SDK. This SDK package depends on other Python packages like pytest, etc. These dependencies are defined in the ```requirements.txt``` file that comes with the SDK. To resolve these dependencies, you can use the PIP Dependency manager. Install it by following steps at [https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/installing/](https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/installing/).

Python and PIP executables should be defined in your PATH. Open command prompt and type ```pip --version```. This should display the version of the PIP Dependency Manager installed if your installation was successful and the paths are properly defined.

* Using command line, navigate to the directory containing the generated files (including ```requirements.txt```) for the SDK.
* Run the command ```pip install -r requirements.txt```. This should install all the required dependencies.
The following section explains how to use the giphyapi library in a new project.
Open up a Python IDE like PyCharm. The basic workflow presented here is also applicable if you prefer using a different editor or IDE.
Click on `Open` in PyCharm to browse to your generated SDK directory and then click `OK`.
The project files will be displayed in the side bar as follows:
Create a new directory by right clicking on the solution name as shown below:

Nodes.AlternateText: Add a new project in PyCharm - Step 1

Nodes.Text: Name the directory as "test".

Nodes.AlternateText: Add a new project in PyCharm - Step 2

Nodes.Text: Add a python file to this project.

Nodes.AlternateText: Add a new project in PyCharm - Step 3

Nodes.Text: Name it "testSDK".

Nodes.AlternateText: Add a new project in PyCharm - Step 4

Nodes.Text: In your python file you will be required to import the generated python library using the following code lines
from giphyapi.giphyapi_client import GiphyapiClient

Nodes.AlternateText: Add a new project in PyCharm - Step 5

Nodes.Text: After this you can write code to instantiate an API client object, get a controller object and  make API calls. Sample code is given in the subsequent sections.
To run the file within your test project, right click on your Python file inside your Test project and click on `Run`
The following parameters are configurable for the API Client:

Data: The Http Client passed from the sdk user for making requests
The value which determines to override properties of the passed Http Client from the sdk user
The callback value that is invoked before and after an HTTP call is made to an endpoint

The value to use for connection timeout.
**Default: 30**

The number of times to retry an endpoint call if it fails.
**Default: 0**

A backoff factor to apply between attempts after the second try.
**Default: 2**

The http methods on which retry is to be done.
**Default: ["GET", "PUT", "GET", "PUT"]**
Optional proxy configuration to route HTTP requests through a proxy server.
The SDK logging configuration for API calls
The credential object for Custom Query Parameter

Nodes.Text: The API client can be initialized as follows:
Code-Based Client Initialization

import logging

from giphyapi.configuration import Environment
from giphyapi.giphyapi_client import GiphyapiClient
from giphyapi.http.auth.custom_query_authentication import CustomQueryAuthenticationCredentials
from giphyapi.logging.configuration.api_logging_configuration import LoggingConfiguration
from giphyapi.logging.configuration.api_logging_configuration import RequestLoggingConfiguration
from giphyapi.logging.configuration.api_logging_configuration import ResponseLoggingConfiguration

client = GiphyapiClient(
    custom_query_authentication_credentials=CustomQueryAuthenticationCredentials(
        api_key='api_key'
    ),
    environment=Environment.PRODUCTION,
    logging_configuration=LoggingConfiguration(
        log_level=logging.INFO,
        request_logging_config=RequestLoggingConfiguration(
            log_body=True
        ),
        response_logging_config=ResponseLoggingConfiguration(
            log_headers=True
        )
    )
)
Environment-Based Client Initialization

from giphyapi.giphyapi_client import GiphyapiClient

# Specify the path to your .env file if it’s located outside the project’s root directory.
client = GiphyapiClient.from_environment(dotenv_path='/path/to/.env')
See the [Environment-Based Client Initialization]($h/__additional_documentation/Configuration/Environment-Based%20Client%20Initialization) section for details.

This API uses the following authentication schemes.
* [`api_key (Custom Query Parameter)`]($h/__auth_CustomQueryAuthentication)
Documentation for accessing and setting credentials for api_key.
**Note:** Auth credentials can be set using `CustomQueryAuthenticationCredentials` object, passed in as named parameter `custom_query_authentication_credentials` in the client initialization.
You must provide credentials in the client as shown in the following code snippet.

from giphyapi.giphyapi_client import GiphyapiClient
from giphyapi.http.auth.custom_query_authentication import CustomQueryAuthenticationCredentials

client = GiphyapiClient(
    custom_query_authentication_credentials=CustomQueryAuthenticationCredentials(
        api_key='api_key'
    )
)

* [Gifs]($e/gifs)
* [Stickers]($e/stickers)
An instance of the `GifsApi` class can be accessed from the API Client.
gifs_api = client.gifs

Fields.Name: bitly_url
Fields.Description: The unique bit.ly URL for this GIF

Fields.Name: content_url
Fields.Description: Currently unused

Fields.Name: create_datetime
Fields.Description: The date this GIF was added to the GIPHY database.

Fields.Name: embded_url
Fields.Description: A URL used for embedding this GIF

Fields.Name: featured_tags
Fields.Description: An array of featured tags for this GIF (Note: Not available when using the Public Beta Key)

Fields.Name: id
Fields.Description: This GIF's unique ID

Fields.Name: images
Fields.Description: An object containing data for various available formats and sizes of this GIF.

Fields.Name: import_datetime
Fields.Description: The creation or upload date from this GIF's source.

Fields.Name: rating
Fields.Description: The MPAA-style rating for this content. Examples include Y, G, PG, PG-13 and R

Fields.Name: slug
Fields.Description: The unique slug used in this GIF's URL

Fields.Name: source
Fields.Description: The page on which this GIF was found

Fields.Name: source_post_url
Fields.Description: The URL of the webpage on which this GIF was found.

Fields.Name: source_tld
Fields.Description: The top level domain of the source URL.

Fields.Name: tags
Fields.Description: An array of tags for this GIF (Note: Not available when using the Public Beta Key)

Fields.Name: trending_datetime
Fields.Description: The date on which this gif was marked trending, if applicable.

Fields.Name: mtype
Fields.Description:
Type of the gif. By default, this is almost always gif

**Default**: `"gif"`

Fields.Name: update_datetime
Fields.Description: The date on which this GIF was last updated.

Fields.Name: url
Fields.Description: The unique URL for this GIF

Fields.Name: user

Nodes.Text: Represents the proxy server configurations for API calls.
Properties

Data: The port to connect to the proxy server.

CodeExamples.Title: Usage Example
CodeExamples.Text:
from giphyapi.giphyapi_client import GiphyapiClient
from giphyapi.http.proxy_settings import ProxySettings

client = GiphyapiClient(
    proxy_settings=ProxySettings(
        address='http://localhost',
        port=8888,
        username='user',
        password='pass'
     )
)

Nodes.Text: The SDK client can also be initialized directly from environment variables using the `from_environment()` class method. This allows the SDK to automatically read configuration values from the runtime environment or a .env file.
Example

from giphyapi.giphyapi_client import GiphyapiClient

# Specify the path to your .env file if it’s located outside the project’s root directory.
client = GiphyapiClient.from_environment(dotenv_path='/path/to/.env')
You can also specify a path to a `.env` file by passing it to the `from_environment()` method:

The same method can accept keyword arguments to override any values read from the environment, and the arguments to override values should follow the same approach as code-based client initialization.

from giphyapi.giphyapi_client import GiphyapiClient

client = GiphyapiClient.from_environment(
    dotenv_path='/path/to/.env',
    timeout=0,  # overrides timeout from environment variable
)
Values provided through arguments take precedence over those defined in environment variables.
Example `.env` File

ENVIRONMENT=production

API_KEY=ExampleApiKey

TIMEOUT=60
MAX_RETRIES=3
BACKOFF_FACTOR=2
RETRY_STATUSES=408,413
RETRY_METHODS=GET,PUT,DELETE

# Logging Configuration
LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG
MASK_SENSITIVE_HEADERS=true

# Request Logging Configuration
REQUEST_LOG_BODY=true
REQUEST_LOG_HEADERS=true
REQUEST_INCLUDE_QUERY_IN_PATH=true
REQUEST_HEADERS_TO_INCLUDE=Content-Type,X-Request-ID
REQUEST_HEADERS_TO_EXCLUDE=Authorization
REQUEST_HEADERS_TO_UNMASK=X-Request-ID

# Response Logging Configuration
RESPONSE_LOG_BODY=true
RESPONSE_LOG_HEADERS=true
RESPONSE_HEADERS_TO_INCLUDE=Content-Type,X-Correlation-ID,Date,Server
RESPONSE_HEADERS_TO_EXCLUDE=Set-Cookie,Authorization,X-API-Key
RESPONSE_HEADERS_TO_UNMASK=X-Correlation-ID

# Proxy Configuration
PROXY_ADDRESS=http://localhost:3000
PROXY_PORT=8080
PROXY_USERNAME=username
PROXY_PASSWORD=password
Note
- If an environment variable is not defined, the default SDK configuration value will be used.
An abstract class for custom logger implementation.
Abstract Methods

Data: Logs a message with a specified log level and additional parameters.

1. level (int): The log level of the message.
2. message (str): The message template to log.
3. params: (Dict[str, Any]): The parameters to include in the log message.

Nodes.Text: Usage Example
Client Initialization with Custom Logger
In order to provide custom logger implementation, the `AbstractLogger` class must be inherited so that you can override the `log` behavior.
Following is how the custom logger implementation can be injected in the SDK client.
Represents the logging configuration for API calls.
Parameters

Data: Takes in your custom implementation of the abstract logger class here. **Default Implementation : `ConsoleLogger`**
Defines the log message severity mentioned in python logging module (e.g., DEBUG, INFO, WARN|WARNING, ERROR, FATAL|CRITICAL). **Default : `logging.INFO`**
Toggles the global setting to mask sensitive HTTP headers in both requests and responses before logging, safeguarding confidential data. **Default : `True`**
The logging configuration for an API request.
The logging configuration for an API response.

Nodes.Text: Represents the logging configuration for API request.
Parameters

Data: Toggles the logging of the request body. **Default : `False`**
Toggles the logging of the request headers. **Default : `False`**
Includes only specified request headers in the log output. **Default : `[]`**
Excludes specified request headers from the log output. **Default : `[]`**
Logs specified request headers without masking, revealing their actual values. **Default : `[]`**
Toggles the inclusion of query parameters in the logged request path. **Default : `False`**

CodeExamples.Title: Usage Example
CodeExamples.Text:
try:
    # Perform API request
    result: ApiResponse = client.example_controller.example_endpoint(input)

    # Inspect response attributes
    print("Status Code:", result.status_code)
    print("Headers:", result.headers)
    print("Body:", result.body)
except APIException as e:
    print("API Exception:", e)
except Exception as e:
    print("General Exception:", e)

Nodes.Title: How quickly can we launch our Developer Portal?

Nodes.Text: You can generate and publish your Developer Portal in minutes. Simply upload your OpenAPI specification, customize the look and feel to match your brand, and publish instantly - either as a standalone site or embedded into your existing Developer Portal.

Nodes.Title: How does the Portal stay current as our API evolves?

Nodes.Text: APIMatic automatically updates your portal when your API specification changes. You can re-import updated API definitions, and the portal will regenerate with the latest endpoints, parameters, and documentation while preserving your customizations.

Nodes.Title: What level of customization and control do we have?

Nodes.Text: You can completely customize the look, feel and behavior of your Developer Experience Portal, including primary colors, themes, custom sections, navigation links, custom URL slugs, and branding. You can also choose to host it on your own custom domain or embed it seamlessly into your existing documentation site using a simple HTML script.

Nodes.Title: Is there a way we can integrate this with our existing Developer Portal?

Nodes.Text: Absolutely. APIMatic provides embeddable portal components that can be seamlessly integrated into your existing documentation sites using a simple HTML script. You can also choose to host the portal separately on your own domain while maintaining your existing portal structure.

Nodes.Title: What AI capabilities does this Developer Portal have?

Nodes.Text: **Context Plugins** let developers connect your API to the AI coding assistant they already use (Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Claude, and more) to search, ask questions, and generate working integration code grounded in your live specification.

The portal also includes a built-in **API Copilot** that answers questions from your API reference and product documentation, and an **AI Assist Menu** that sends any documentation page straight to your preferred AI tool as context with one click.
